scenario: Before each production deployment, the team wants to verify the BPMN against best practices and Zeebe execution rules -- no Service Task without retries, no orphan flows, etc. They want this in the CI pipeline.
question: Which approach fits?

options.text: Manual review by the lead developer before each production deployment. Manual code review catches many issues but does not scale as the number of processes and deployments grows. Human reviewers may miss subtle rule violations such as missing retry configurations or orphaned sequence flows. Integrating bpmnlint into the CI pipeline provides automated, consistent, and exhaustive validation on every pull request without relying on human availability.

Deploy without validation and wait for failures in the production environment to detect BPMN issues. Production is not a testing environment, and discovering best-practice violations or execution errors in production causes customer-facing incidents. Camunda 8 bpmnlint provides rule-based design-time validation that catches issues before any deployment, making this reactive approach both wasteful and risky for production process deployments.

Use bpmnlint with Camunda-specific rule sets integrated into the CI pipeline -- run on each pull request and fail the build on violations. Web Modeler embeds bpmnlint for real-time design-time feedback. Custom and community Camunda rule packs include checks for missing retries on Service Tasks, orphaned sequence flows, and other Zeebe-specific requirements. The CI integration enforces these rules automatically at code-review time.

BPMN validation tooling requires the Modeler UI and has no CLI form that can integrate into CI pipelines -- so the claim goes. This is factually incorrect. js-capable CI environment. It runs as a command-line tool, accepts BPMN file paths as arguments, and returns exit codes that CI systems use to pass or fail pipeline stages.

a.text: Incorrect. bpmnlint is a community/Camunda-recommended lint tool with rule sets for Zeebe deployment. Integrate into CI for design-time guardrails.

b.text: Incorrect. Manual review is necessary but not sufficient.

c.text: Correct. Production isn't a test environment.
